title: Cirrus: Migrate off of PAPR + Improve testing
body: Replace PAPR with 'in_podman' / Cirrus-CI based test.  Prepare the groundwork for replacing Homu and Travis with Bors + VM-based testing (set "optional" in this PR).   Once this PR is merged, until #2115 is merged: maintainers must carefully/manually merge PRs one-by-one (since the Homu bot will be disabled).

comments.body: Thanks @cevich looking forward to this working. and I am sure @jlebon is looking forward to it also.  :^)
okay, basic stuff is all working well, now to actually execute some tests :smile:
note to me:   somehow things running in podman are not returning exit codes?
This looks like the cgroup file system is being mounted readonly into the container that is running podman, or on the host?

I've looked and looked but I cannot see where it's mounting up cgroups anything into the container.  The host is a F30 VM, not too different from what we use in libpod.  Searching,  I find is that `-e "CGROUP_MANAGER=cgroupfs"` option from the `in_podman` function I linked.  Could that be triggering test-behavior that shouldn't be enabled?
Can you ssh into the box?

Yes, we have the same `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` script here and ability to 'delete-protect' VMs.  I agree, that seems a reasonable next step.  The other thought I had (though a bit undesirable) is to temporarily skip the offenders to support the migration.  Then pick up immediately on fixing them.  Either way, lemmie grab one of these fetched-up VMs so we can poke it with sharpened sticks...

Oh...and this is probably an easy answer:  For F30 w/ `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs STORAGE_OPT=vfs.mountopt=nodev` I see this for a few tests:

```
[+1642s] # time="2019-10-23T17:00:15Z" level=debug msg="[graphdriver] trying provided driver \"vfs\""
[+1642s] # time="2019-10-23T17:00:15Z" level=error msg="error opening storage: vfs driver does not support overlay.mountopt options"
```

That seems like a bug to me (either in test, or elsewhere), eh?

Ref, the important beginning parts:

```
[root@cevich-fedora-cloud-base-30-1-2-1565360543 buildah]# contrib/cirrus/test.sh integration
rm -f -r buildah imgtype build buildah.static buildah.darwin tests/testreport/testreport

...cut...
```

(confirming it fails in the same way)

Ref: command used to execute in papr: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/.papr.yml#L50-L53
trying with a `-v /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup`

```
not ok 19 blobcache-build-compressed-using-dockerfile-explicit-push
```

Remove STORAGE_OPT=vfs.mountopt=nodev
Why are these tests difference then before?
Where does this podman command come from?  I don't see it under Buildah?
it's not SELinux.  I tried both `permissive` and `setsebool container_manage_cgroup true`.

> Where does this podman command come from? I don't see it under Buildah?

Ugg, trying and failing to give useful links, sorry.  It comes from `contrib/cirrus/lib.sh` if github would behave for me...

Papr was doing it like:

```
podman run --net=host --security-opt label=disable --cap-add all --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -v /etc/yum.repos.d:/etc/yum.repos.d.host:ro
    -v $PWD:/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah
    --workdir /go/src/github.com/containers/buildah
    registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:30 bash -c sh ./.papr.sh
```

(which didn't work, so I, err, started adding things :grin: )

> Why are these tests difference then before?

@rhatdan I think one of the things we're dealing with is the vast difference in environments and podman.  Papr was last successful running (command above) on a Fedora Atomic 28 host.  So presumably buildah development stuck with what ran and passed there (i.e. using a very old podman).  Now with a F30 host and brand-new-fresh podman, test-vs-host expectations are just not lining up any more.

> Remove STORAGE_OPT=vfs.mountopt=nodev

I'll try again, but IIRC w/o that the tests throw errors complaining about `overlay.mountopts` (despite STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs`)

So it seems the `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` and `rootless` tasks without `STORAGE_OPT=vfs.mountopt=nodev` are what throws

```
[+1602s] # time="2019-10-24T20:30:05Z" level=debug msg="[graphdriver] trying provided driver \"vfs\""
[+1602s] # time="2019-10-24T20:30:05Z" level=error msg="error opening storage: vfs driver does not support overlay.mountopt options"
```

`BUILDAH_ISOLATION=` (empty)  task gives the `/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah324011388: read-only file system\`  cgroup errors. 

There are some other failures mixed in which I'm ignoring for now.  In any case, based on the huge number of tests that do pass, overall this PR is closer than it was on Monday.
I think maybe the next step is to see if I can manually reproduce the mount-options thing...perhaps there's a bug there.

@rhatdan I can reproduce the read-only cgroup directory thing locally (F29) using a simplified version of the container used in these tests:

```
# podman run -it --rm --net=host --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --cap-add=all -e CGROUP_MANAGER=cgroupfs -e STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs -v /dev/fuse:/dev/fuse fedora:latest mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah364534981

mkdir: cannot create directory '/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah364534981': Read-only file system
```

I produce the same error using a simplified version of the former PAPR container command:

```
# podman run -it --rm --net=host --security-opt label=disable --cap-add all --security-opt seccomp=unconfined fedora:latest mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah364534981

mkdir: cannot create directory '/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah364534981': Read-only file system
```

and for sanity-sake, the `mkdir` does work directly on my host:

```
# mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah364534981
# rmdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/buildah-buildah364534981
```

@rhatdan I chatted with Matt about this.  We couldn't get any definitive answers, but he seems to believe it's possible we had a read-write cgroups in podman during the F28 timeframe.  Since these containers are running with super-privileges anyway, he confirmed our best bet is to just volume-mount `/sys/fs/cgroup` read-write in the containers.

I suppose the only other choice here is to dive into a bunch of old tests and re-write as needed.  I'm not prepared for that nor would I feel good asking someone else to do it :face_with_head_bandage:

(Rebased + added ***rw*** mounting of cgroups, enabling `container_manage_cgroup`, plus added a few comments)

Hopefully fixing the cgroups problem will leave only the `overlay.mountopts` problem to address...

@rhatdan the `overlay.mountopt` issue I think is an actual bug.  I'm able to reproduce it easily:

```
$ sudo podman run --net=host --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --cap-add=all -e CGROUP_MANAGER=cgroupfs -e STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs -v /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup:rw -it --rm fedora:latest bash

[root@cevich ~]# dnf install -y buildah
...cut...
 buildah                        x86_64        1.11.3-2.fc30                         updates        8.6 M
...cut...
Complete!
[root@cevich ~]# buildah from scratch
vfs driver does not support overlay.mountopt options
[root@cevich ~]# buildah --storage-driver=vfs from scratch
working-container
```

IIRC we had an issue similar to this in podman, do you remember the details?

I have a PR to begin fixing this in the works, actually merged into container/storage. 
The issue is we do not clear the STORAGE_OPTS when STORAGE_DRIVER is set, we do if the user overrides via command line.

You need to add -e STORAGE_OPTIONS="" and I think everything will work.

Here's the issue: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1783 and https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3240 (in podman)

So should I be setting `STORAGE_OPT=overlay.mountopt=`?  That just seems wrong (we're not using 'overlay', we're using 'vfs')
Won't STORAGE_OPTIONS="" work?

> Won't STORAGE_OPTIONS="" work?

Nope, same deal.  I'm convinced this is simply buildah's flavor of containers/libpod#3240

The quick-fix there was to comment-out `mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"` in `/etc/containers/storage.conf`.  

Opened #1945
(force-pushed w/ quick-fix to `mountopt` issue + squashed up commits)
(rebased w/ addition of /dev/fuse volume-mount to podman command-line)

```
[+0809s] # error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c echo this-should-be-cached-but-it-s-not]: time="2019-10-28T14:50:44Z" level=warning msg="exit status 1"
```

That seems like maybe a bug in the tests or buildah, if it really is trying to (simulate?) rootless mode.
Looks like the user namespace is not being setup.

@edsantiago you mentioned that you were looking over some buildah BATs tests or results.  It's likely a long-shot, but sometimes I get lucky:  Are any of the failures above familiar or one's you've seen in the past maybe?

I tried my best to troubleshoot this, but the root cause appears as a complex interaction between several scripts, the CI system(s), and Makefile.  I'd like to make a concerted effort to fix this, but am also juggling other competing priority work :confused:  (I may not get to it before others start complaining)

I wonder if removing Papr is causing homu to blow up?  Might need to force commit.
:hourglass: Testing commit 32c9595 with merge 6b47de3...
:boom: Test timed out

Right, Homu watches for the `required` context, which [PAPR can set](https://github.com/projectatomic/papr/blob/c13f38f32448cd31d858b52704b5715104ac08a0/docs/sample.papr.yml#L106).

Not sure if there's a way to replicate this semantic with Cirrus CI, though if you just want to "merge when all the checks are green", it might be easier to move over to Prow like libpod did.

> Not sure if there's a way to replicate this semantic with Cirrus CI

Cirrus touches the new Status API, so a workaround would be to add add a final task to touch `required`, then I think Homu could work.  This isn't a small amount of effort though, and all solutions in this space require giving a bot account repository-write access (somewhere in the chain).  My preference would be to use Github-Actions, but that's only because I don't/can't understand how prow works and native solutions are almost always better than third-parties.

In any case, I think this is work for a follow-up PR, so we should either ignore or disable Homu for now.
@cevich so you are saying to manually merge?  Will this break humu for all future PRs?

> Will this break humu for all future PRs?

Yes until fixed or worked around and/or I understand more of Homu's configuration.  Would you prefer that I address/include a fix/workaround in this PR?  (instead of a separate PR)

Details:

Cirrus-CI will automatically run the tests on the target branch after a PR merges.  This is (for example, in libpod CI) how a notification is sent to `#podman` on IRC when post-merge tests fail.  Homu mitigates this post-merge test-failure possibility a different way, since it controls the merging of all PRs, it can first make sure the tests pass after rebasing from the destination branch.

The down-side of this approach is, since Homu is the gatekeeper, if it ever breaks, nothing gets in.  It's also (necessarily) serializing all merge operations so there's a performance impact on a busy repo (like libpod) with a rapidly moving branch.  I can poke around and see if there's a github-actions way of doing this, that may be a simpler solution.

Ref: Good explanation of 'evergreen builds': https://github.com/bors-ng/bors-ng#but-dont-githubs-protected-branches-already-do-this

Even with Prow in charge, post-merge testing still fails on occasion, so it's not a perfect solution.  

Assuming this repo. is much lower traffic than libpod, another idea is:  We turn on the 'must be up-to-date' setting for PRs.  That forces them to always merge serially, by placing the rebase effort on PR authors (which is why it's bad for a busy repo)
Does the must be up2date mean that everyone needs to rebase or can anyone press a button on github to refire the tests?

> Does the must be up2date mean that everyone needs to rebase

Yes, it is that annoying.  One PR merges and it forces all others to have to rebase/repush (and so, re-test) before merging.  IMHO, it's not too horrible on a low-traffic repo., this one seems close to the edge of too-much.  It's easy to turn on/off though w/o much consequence, so low-cost to try it.

I poked around some more today at other solutions, but can't even really see a way to do this 100% in native github-actions - they all run async/parallel by nature and so need a third-party "arbitrator" to (effectively) serialize the stream of merging PRs and handle possible test-failures.

I really wish there was a simple solution (even a half-way "good enough" one).  I'm not above kludging Cirrus to behave with Homu - except for not wanting to burden jlebon any more than necessary (including ongoing maintenance).  So I'm willing to try poking at the Prow dragon again, see if I can make sense of what's needed to hook it up.  I'm also willing to keep looking and see if I can learn more / discover other possibilities.

Hmmm, there does appear to be a public-repo supporting github-app for bors-ng (bors is what Homu is based on): https://github.com/apps/bors

It's a free-for-opensource github-app that "someone else" manages the server/database stuff.  I have a mind to look into that a bit more, since the docs suggest it plays nice with github-actions (and therefor cirrus-ci).

Hrmmm, I really like what I'm seeing with https://bors.tech/, it's a much larger and active community, hosts a free service for OSS projects, and supports all the new APIs.  Most importantly WRT Cirrus-CI, they support wild-card matching status names.  So for example, we could make these required:

```testing image:fedora-*```
```testing image:ubuntu-18*```

but ignore the status of others (e.g. experimental new tests).  Let me do some more reading/playing, perhaps it could replace Homu here and knowing how it works would let me rig up other repos as well...

oooohhhh! It's travis. ok.  @rhatdan can I make travis __not__ required for merge or is it too early for that?
